cot_prefix: Okay, let me read through the text again to make sure I understand the question correctly. The question is asking when an ordinary tropical storm is called a hurricane. So the key point here is that when the winds reach 75 miles per hour or more, the storm is called a hurricane. Option D is "When its winds reach 75 miles per hour." That directly matches the information in the text. Let me check the other options to be sure. Option A is "When it begins in the Atlantic and Caribbean seas." The text mentions that storms are born in those areas, but not all of them become hurricanes. So A isn't the right answer. Option B is "When it hits the coastline."
